mapping.a_element: path log-likelihood / Radon–Nikodym derivative of the path law
mapping.b_element: predictive log-likelihood of a Gaussian or mixture density head

mapping.a_element: relative entropy (conditional mutual information) scoring departure from a product law
mapping.b_element: proper scoring rules (CRPS, pinball) scoring the predictive distribution's fit

brief_justification: Paper A is a measure-theoretic path-space framework for detecting factorisation / conditional independence of stochastic histories via Girsanov changes of measure, Radon–Nikodym derivatives, relative entropy and Schrödinger-bridge control, whereas Paper B is an empirical benchmark of neural forecasting components (backbones vs. output heads) for fat-tailed returns judged by CRPS/pinball; the only overlap is the generic presence of probability densities and log-likelihoods, not any shared governing process or role-for-role mechanism.
